visible_workflow: select one clear topic or niche
generate a multi-slide copy draft in one pass
human editor fixes the hook, problem-solution logic, length, and repetitive tone
retain facts only, verify sources, and remove AI-sounding language
visible_principles: topic quality is half the work
automation handles drafting rather than final judgment
project_value: Direct reference for our CardNews planning and editorial QA flow.
The source's eight-page example is a batch shape, not a fixed slide requirement for this project.

items.visible_problem: A vague request such as make an Instagram card-news post produces the same product-introduction, three-benefit, purchase-prompt structure repeatedly.
failure_modes: same structure across products and topics
insufficient reason to save or share
information summary without narrative, evidence, or account character
items.project_value: Useful negative reference explaining why a generic generation prompt cannot be the production pipeline.

tools.name: OpenShell sandbox
tools.visible_claim: launch Claude, OpenCode, Codex, or Copilot in an isolated container using a similar CLI form
tools.possible_value: reduce environment collision and bound experimental agents

tools.name: SkillOpt
tools.visible_claim: treat skill text as optimization parameters, propose add/delete/replace changes, and apply only changes that improve evaluation scores
tools.possible_value: reference architecture for evidence-based skill evolution instead of manual prompt drift

items.guardrail: Neither tool was inspected, installed, connected, or evaluated in this batch.

tools.name: pocket-tts
tools.visible_claim: CPU-only text-to-speech with fast first audio output and long-text processing
tools.possible_value: future local narration fallback for approved Shorts work

tools.name: OmniRoute
tools.possible_value: possible provider routing and fallback abstraction

items.guardrail: Shorts expansion, model-provider routing, and external API use remain approval-gated; visible performance numbers are unverified.

visible_claims: many free-tier and paid providers collected behind one dashboard
pool and deduplication logic for reported free token availability
automatic routing when one provider reaches a limit
project_value: Architectural reference for a provider-neutral fallback layer.
Could reduce one-provider lock-in only if privacy, keys, data routing, reliability, and cost controls pass review.
items.guardrail: Do not connect unknown free providers or route project data through them without a security and approval review.

visible_claims: one-line CLI workflow for presentations and office documents
agent skill installation for Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and Copilot
unifies python-pptx, openpyxl, and python-docx style workflows
project_value: Reference for export automation and polished report or deck delivery.
The sample slide demonstrates strong geometric color blocking, negative space, and concise data chips.
items.guardrail: Current project and workspace document tooling should be checked before adding a duplicate dependency.

items.topic: Cowork cloud execution and usage data claims
visible_claims: sessions continue with their context rather than receiving only remote instructions
reported usage is concentrated in business processes and content creation
cloud agent tools are shifting toward non-developer business users
items.project_value: Reference for separating persistent work sessions from one-shot remote commands and for designing owner-friendly operations.
items.guardrail: The dataset and product claims were not independently checked.

items.topic: Cowork cloud session persistence and background work
visible_claims: sessions and files are stored with the account and continue across devices
scheduled work may run while the local computer is off
execution moves from local desktop dependency to cloud infrastructure
project_value: Useful conceptual reference for continuity across CTO handoffs and device state.
Any cloud move would require explicit data-boundary, cost, approval, and reliability decisions.

visible_claims: contact-sheet grid combines keyframes into three images
local viewer shows video, keyframes, and transcript in one screen
project_value: High-value reference for future Shorts review and current visual-asset inspection.
A contact sheet reduces repeated opening of individual frames and helps compare narrative progression quickly.
The same concept can be adapted to CardNews three-by-three grid previews and variable-slide contact sheets.

items.visible_value: Persist plan, discoveries, and progress on disk so a later session can continue after context loss.
project_value: Directly relevant to preventing another handoff where work exists only in chat context.
This project already has operating, work-order, status, and handoff documents; the concept should map into those files instead of creating conflicting duplicate status systems.
items.guardrail: Do not install a second planning convention or let workers overwrite CTO-owned status documents without an integration decision.

cross_batch_learning: CardNews automation should accelerate research-backed drafts, not remove editorial judgment.
Generic create-a-card-news prompts produce repetitive low-save-value output.
Skill evolution is valuable only when evaluation evidence controls promotion.
Provider aggregation can improve resilience but creates security, privacy, and data-routing risk.
Contact sheets and unified visual viewers can reduce review effort for both variable CardNews and future Shorts.
Persistent work files are essential, but this project should preserve one authoritative status-document system.
